239185.explanation: As long as Bernheze remains financially healthy like it is now and as long as we as a municipal government stay close to our residents and entrepreneurs, we consider joining a larger municipality (even partly) unnecessary and undesirable.

239186.explanation: An advisory referendum can be helpful in exceptional cases and under conditions for decision-making. Moreover, elections already provide a democratically elected representative body, there is resident participation, and there are various opportunities for public input.

239187.explanation: The municipality must monitor whether everything is properly maintained by residents after a takeover, which is expected to cause unwanted additional costs, and we do not want that.

239190.explanation: Within the prescribed opening hours of the General Local Ordinance (APV), they can decide when to be open themselves. Outside these hours, they cannot, for example to prevent (noise) nuisance at night.

239191.explanation: Agricultural land can be converted into nature reserves where location and limited use permit. However, we do not want to mandate this because of claims that would cause the land price to fall.

239192.explanation: This is a nice diversification and extra opportunity for entrepreneurs. The market and competition (supply and demand) will naturally ensure the right balance in the number of stores/campsites.

239193.explanation: This can be done, for example, with an (interest-free) loan repaid within a set period, making it easier to start such projects. Using energy storage can also relieve the power grid, creating new possibilities.

239195.explanation: This is primarily the responsibility of new residents themselves; they must make the greatest effort to integrate into Bernheze. The municipality’s role is mainly to ensure a good and safe environment where integration can happen quickly.

239197.explanation: With proper information and the help of the 'stookwijzer' (heating guide), biomass such as wood can responsibly be used for heating. We believe this assessment should be made further by the national government.

239198.explanation: We strongly prefer solar panels on roofs. However, there are good opportunities to install solar panels on land while still allowing good agricultural use, for example with movable or partially transparent panels.

239199.explanation: Financial support can be provided under certain conditions to families with young children so those children can fully participate with their peers.

239201.explanation: Attention to music and culture belongs in the curriculum. Through municipal subsidies to, for example, Eijnderic or Muzelinck, music lessons are accessible to our residents.

239202.explanation: We like to support sports clubs where possible so that important facilities are preserved, even when times are tough.

239203.explanation: Monuments shape the landscape and also tell about the history and identity of Bernheze; we want to preserve them for future generations.

239204.explanation: Strengthening the police is a better option. This reduces police response times, and with an additional community officer, visibility and safety improve.

239205.explanation: This could happen by appointment so that residents and entrepreneurs can easily report undermining activities (crime) or other worrying situations.

239207.explanation: We must look at the need for social rental housing per village. This does not automatically mean it must always be 30%.

239208.explanation: Adding an extra floor to apartments allows more homes to be built on the same footprint for our residents. We want to allow this only in suitable locations.

239210.explanation: Holiday homes are not intended for this purpose; their use and designation should correspond to residential housing. We prefer holiday homes to be used for tourism and recreation.

239212.explanation: Bernheze is not a student city, and this kind of situation clouds the visibility of who lives where, which can lead to unsafe situations.

239213.explanation: We support people in poverty on multiple fronts as a municipality and assist everyone living in poverty. We strive to get people structurally above the poverty line as quickly as possible.

239214.explanation: We think it is good if they contribute to society appropriately where possible. Often they are willing to do so, which helps them better integrate into society.

239215.explanation: We believe it is better to guide people towards more work/a better income so they can fully participate in society. This also means that there are rights and obligations, such as paying taxes.

239216.explanation: By following additional training, their chances of finding a job often increase, enabling them to work again sooner, and applying for jobs helps with that.

239218.explanation: We have a parking standard based on the average number of vehicles per household. Providing fewer parking spaces than this standard causes nuisance in residential neighborhoods. Parking spaces can also be made with grass pavers for a greener neighborhood.

239220.explanation: A lower speed limit for fast traffic creates a safer situation for slow traffic such as cyclists and pedestrians. It also results in less noise, vibrations, and disturbance. At the edges of built-up areas, a speed of 50 km/h may still be desirable.

239221.explanation: The healthcare system is for everyone. If there are personal additional requirements or wishes, this care can be purchased at one's own expense.

239222.explanation: The quality of youth care is very important. Prevention and/or avoiding further problems is always better for young people and thus also ensures lower overall costs.

239224.explanation: The absence of a recycling center likely leads to more illegal waste dumping. However, we can improve accessibility by, for example, collaborating with surrounding municipalities where residents of Bernheze are also allowed to bring their waste to the recycling center.

239226.explanation: It is not intended that sports canteens of sports clubs that receive subsidies from the municipality are used for parties and celebrations. This is unwanted and inappropriate competition with hospitality businesses.

239227.explanation: The size difference of a club should not automatically mean that a club no longer receives a subsidy, because smaller clubs can also make a valuable contribution to society.

239236.explanation: Community centers are a valuable facility where residents come together for socio-cultural activities. We want them to be able to operate sustainably on their own and support them where necessary.

239237.explanation: The municipality is easily accessible by phone, digitally, or by letter. Questions from less digitally skilled people can always, for example, be asked by telephone.

239238.explanation: We closely follow national developments and new laws and regulations. At the same time, we want to prevent housing construction from coming to a halt in Bernheze. The choice of where to live—next to a highway, in nature, or near a livestock farm—is a personal decision.

239239.explanation: A proportional distribution based on the need per village is important to realize sufficient social rental housing based on supply and demand.

239241.explanation: This is not desirable everywhere. It can cause a lot of extra traffic due to visitors and loading and unloading of products. Therefore, we do not find it wise to allow this in residential neighborhoods.

239242.explanation: It is important that newcomers integrate well, and language is an important part of this as one of the rights and duties.

239243.explanation: Safe cycling routes are important facilities in a municipality like Bernheze. This can include, for example, an express cycle route, wider cycle paths, or installing additional lighting on frequently used routes.

239244.explanation: The possible bypass roads are intended within or adjacent to the built-up area; a speed limit of 50 km/h is appropriate here to prevent noise nuisance and increase safety.

239247.explanation: We believe there is insufficient clarity and support from The Hague regarding asylum reception concerning safety, (mental) healthcare, and guidance. We therefore find it irresponsible to realize asylum reception now without clear guidelines and support.

239248.explanation: We believe there is insufficient clarity and support from The Hague regarding asylum reception concerning safety, (mental) healthcare, and guidance. We therefore find it irresponsible to realize asylum reception now without clear guidelines and support.

239185.explanation: We are a financially healthy municipality and can manage independently. We remain autonomous and contribute to regional cooperation.

239186.explanation: Our principle is "We do it together." That means we actively involve residents and entrepreneurs in important decisions. A referendum can help with this.

239187.explanation: Yes, certainly. Letting go in trust when good ideas and plans exist. We do it together, and residents are given space to take over municipal tasks. In our view, evaluation moments should be held for all parties involved.

239190.explanation: The current arrangement offers enough space. If entrepreneurs want more possibilities, we are happy to discuss it. We find it important that the opinions of both entrepreneurs and residents are considered.

239191.explanation: We need land for food production, but if farmers stop, converting it into nature reserves is an option based on voluntariness. It is NOT mandatory but is an option.

239192.explanation: That really depends on the location of the farm and the function involved. We are always open to discuss ideas and see what is possible.

239193.explanation: We support residents who want to generate sustainable energy together. Local energy projects are important for the energy transition.

239195.explanation: Integration is a collective effort, not just the municipality's responsibility. Therefore, it is important that newcomers quickly find their place in our villages.

239197.explanation: In Bernheze, the use of open fireplaces and wood stoves should remain possible. We want to help residents learn more about safe and comfortable burning. For example, about times when wood burning has more impact on the environment. This way, everyone can make more conscious choices.

239198.explanation: We do not want a total ban. We choose dual use of the land. Solar energy is urgently needed for the energy transition.

239199.explanation: Everyone must be able to participate. We help people with little money so that membership fees are not a barrier.

239201.explanation: It is important that all children can receive music lessons, and there are various (subsidized) options via Muzelinck, the fanfares, and the harmonies.

239202.explanation: Sports clubs play an important role in our villages: they bring people together, strengthen social contacts, and provide residents of all ages a place to meet, exercise, and participate. Therefore, we find it important to support them when necessary.

239203.explanation: We are proud of our monuments. That is why we do not want to cut budgets here.

239204.explanation: The police are responsible for public order and safety. Within Bernheze, we have an experienced team of enforcement officers (boa's).

239205.explanation: The community police officer must be visible and accessible. A weekly consultation hour in each village can help with this.

239207.explanation: We agree with the goal of 30% social housing in Bernheze. The need may differ per village. We take that into account.

239208.explanation: This can differ per village. In larger villages, this may be appropriate. Additionally, we find it important that new building plans are made as much as possible in consultation with residents and align with the needs per core.

239210.explanation: A holiday home is a holiday home, intended for recreation. We do not use them as permanent addresses.

239212.explanation: The housing shortage is high, so it must be possible. We find it important to look for out-of-the-box solutions to this problem, but it must be safe.

239213.explanation: No one may end up in a difficult situation. We support residents in poverty where necessary.

239214.explanation: We want to encourage this and realize that it is not always possible.

239215.explanation: No one may end up in a difficult situation. We do it together. Residents who cannot pay should not be burdened additionally. Waiving municipal taxes for low incomes remains necessary.

239216.explanation: First train or retrain, then guide to work. Not having to apply for jobs during training temporarily helps people get started.

239218.explanation: There are more and more cars. We want a realistic number of parking spaces. Too few spots cause nuisance.

239220.explanation: Where possible, 30 km/h is good for safety. Where the situation allows, 50 km/h should remain permissible in our view.

239221.explanation: Everyone should feel comfortable with the care they receive.

239222.explanation: Quality is paramount. When young people need help, we want good care. Price is not the most important factor.

239224.explanation: Do not close them. Residents should be able to bring their waste to our own municipality.

239226.explanation: Sports canteens are for sports and clubs. For parties and weddings, there are hospitality venues.

239227.explanation: We want healthy clubs where there is demand. The number of members is not the only thing that counts and is not decisive for us.

239236.explanation: We do not want that. Village halls are important as meeting places. We want to preserve and support them.

239237.explanation: Basically, everything is digitally accessible and for a physical counter you are welcome at the town hall.

239238.explanation: We do not consider this desirable for health reasons.

239239.explanation: The distribution must match the needs of each village. As the municipality of Bernheze, we want to achieve a minimum of 30% social housing.

239241.explanation: That depends on the location and the activity. It must not cause nuisance.

239242.explanation: Mastering the Dutch language is the most important condition to be able to participate in our society.

239243.explanation: Safe connections with good lighting between our villages are a must. Especially for our youth.

239244.explanation: The bypass aims to be a faster connection around the villages. 50 km per hour does not fit with that.

239247.explanation: This location has been proposed as an option by the College. Whether and when an asylum seekers' center will actually be built here is not yet clear. In the development of these plans, we as the municipal council want to keep control, for example by setting clear conditions (such as in terms of safety).

239248.explanation: We are in favor of one location where everything is well arranged and all conditions (such as safety) are met.

239185.explanation: The SP wants Bernheze to remain independent. Bernheze is a strong municipality. In the event of a possible redivision, residents have the final say through a binding referendum.

239186.explanation: The SP wants residents to have a say on important issues through a referendum. There must really be a choice, no sham referenda.

239187.explanation: The SP wants to encourage and make space for citizen initiatives. Residents who want to take responsibility are supported.

239190.explanation: The SP wants to maintain the current opening hours. These are more than sufficient. On special occasions, exemptions for extended hours are already possible.

239191.explanation: The SP wants to cooperate with farmers on a sustainable future. No forced conversion, but space for nature development where farmers want it.

239192.explanation: The SP wants to support farmers who want to switch. However, we keep an eye on suitability and support base. Not 'always', but there is room.

239193.explanation: The SP wants the municipality itself to take the lead in generating and storing sustainable energy. That is the government's task. Financial support for individual residents goes too far for a municipality alone.

239195.explanation: The SP wants status holders and labor migrants to know their way in our villages and participate in club life. We stimulate this, but it does not have to cost extra money. In the long term, it actually saves costs. We use the available budgets to help these people.

239197.explanation: The SP wants to counter nuisance from wood burning through information campaigns based on the Stookwijzer. Furthermore, we take advantage of the space that the national government offers us to take additional measures.

239198.explanation: The SP wants to minimize solar fields on agricultural land. Agricultural land is for food production. However, banning it is not up to local authorities.

239199.explanation: The SP wants that lack of money is never a reason not to participate in sports. We support people with little money to keep participating in sports clubs.

239201.explanation: The SP wants every child to have the chance to discover creative talents. Connections between culture and education are important. The municipality already stimulates and subsidizes this through Muzelink.

239202.explanation: The SP wants associations that get into trouble to be able to count on the municipality to think along.

239203.explanation: The SP wants to be careful with the monuments in our municipality.

239204.explanation: The SP wants sufficient neighborhood officers and enforcement officers in Bernheze. Safety is important.

239205.explanation: The SP wants the police to be visibly present in neighborhoods and villages. How the police do that is up to the police themselves.

239207.explanation: The SP wants to make concrete agreements with housing corporations and developers about numbers. Thirty percent social housing is a hard minimum.

239208.explanation: The SP wants to allow houses of four stories or more where appropriate. The need for new, affordable housing is high.

239210.explanation: The SP wants holiday homes not to be lived in permanently. They are not intended or suitable for permanent residence. Sometimes temporary use is needed, but never as a permanent solution.

239212.explanation: The SP wants to enable room rental and shared housing as part of the solution to the housing crisis. What we do not want is subdivision for labor migrants.

239213.explanation: The SP wants to create an action plan against poverty modeled on Apeldoorn. Actively reach out to people, not wait until they ask for help.

239214.explanation: The SP wants to encourage volunteer work and low-regulation social assistance with room to earn side income. But no coercion.

239215.explanation: The SP wants to spare people with low incomes. Tenants do not pay sewage charges and that will remain so.

239216.explanation: The SP wants people who want to develop themselves to get the chance to do so. Further training is an investment in the future.

239218.explanation: The SP wants affordable alternatives to the car first, such as public transportation. Until then, it is unwise to focus on reducing parking spaces.

239220.explanation: The SP wants to improve traffic safety, especially around schools. We want children to be able to cycle or walk safely to school.

239221.explanation: The SP wants care to fit the person. Freedom of choice in care is important for quality of life.

239222.explanation: The SP wants the advice of the involved specialist to be leading for help with youth facing more serious problems. We choose the best provider, not the cheapest.

239224.explanation: The SP wants to keep separate waste collection important. Closing the recycling center leads to more waste on the streets and in nature.

239226.explanation: The SP wants to trust the own responsibility of associations. We assume that (sports) clubs also take responsibility regarding alcohol use among youth. When necessary, we will also address associations about this.

239227.explanation: The SP wants to support associations. Associations play an important role and deserve support, not budget cuts.

239236.explanation: The SP wants to help community centers keep up with the times. Community centers are the beating heart of our villages. Modern, attractive, and accessible.

239237.explanation: The SP wants the municipality to remain accessible to everyone. Personal contact where needed, not only digital.

239238.explanation: The SP does not want goat farms within one kilometer of a village. Therefore, we focus on relocating or stopping goat farms within one kilometer of a village.

239239.explanation: The SP wants enough affordable housing in every village. We aim for thirty percent social housing in every community, though the priority lies in our larger villages.

239241.explanation: The SP wants to keep shops in village centers. We want to keep neighborhoods livable. Shops at homes also attract traffic.

239242.explanation: The SP wants to support people in development and integration, not punish them. A positive approach works better.

239243.explanation: The SP wants safe connections between villages. Improving bike connections is important.

239244.explanation: The SP wants safety to remain important, but the speed limit depends on the specific situation and layout of the road.

239247.explanation: The SP wants to use Zevenbergen 10 as a location for asylum seeker reception. As a municipality, we have the task to make reception possible. The location is already designated. Moreover, the location aligns with the wishes of residents who filled out the survey.

239248.explanation: The SP wants small-scale reception, but the location must also be able to provide facilities on site. This is also derived from the survey our residents completed. The smaller the location, the simpler (and more expensive) the facilities are.

239185.explanation: Progressief Bernheze wants Bernheze to remain independent for as long as possible. But not at any cost. If services worsen or finances come under pressure, we are open to reorganization. Good governance for residents takes priority.

239186.explanation: Progressief Bernheze believes residents should have a say in decisions about their municipality. For important matters, a referendum can help make a fair decision together. This actively involves residents in local democracy.

239187.explanation: Progressief Bernheze believes residents should be able to take over management of a sports hall or park. When people receive trust and support, good ideas arise. With funding, knowledge, and materials, involvement in the neighborhood grows.

239190.explanation: Progressief Bernheze trusts local entrepreneurs. They know when their customers come. Extended opening hours ensure lively villages. We do make clear agreements so neighbors do not experience nuisance.

239191.explanation: Progressief Bernheze wants the municipality to help convert farmland into nature. With flowers along fields, less mowing, and support for nature projects, every piece of land can contribute to more plants, animals and nature in Bernheze.

239192.explanation: Progressief Bernheze believes farmers should be allowed to start extra activities such as a shop or campsite. This way they can earn their income. At the same time, we work with farmers on more sustainable agriculture with less pollution and smaller livestock farms.

239193.explanation: Progressief Bernheze believes residents who generate sustainable energy together should get support. Energy cooperatives provide green and affordable electricity, more control, and encourage people to use energy more consciously and efficiently.

239195.explanation: Progressief Bernheze finds it important to invest in a good start for newcomers. Through help with language, work, and participation in associations, they can find their place in our villages faster.

239197.explanation: Progressief Bernheze thinks fireplaces and wood stoves may remain. However, we want less nuisance and fewer health problems. Therefore, we focus on good information, temporary burning bans during bad weather, and enforcement if there is nuisance.

239198.explanation: Progressief Bernheze does not want to ban solar panels on farmland. They provide clean energy and can help nature, with flowers or small grazers under the panels. However, we prioritize solar panels on roofs and are cautious as long as roofs remain unused.

239199.explanation: Progressief Bernheze believes everyone should be able to do sports. For people with little money, especially children, money should not be a barrier. Therefore, the municipality must help pay membership fees so everyone can participate.

239201.explanation: Progressief Bernheze believes every child should be able to have music lessons. Music supports development and relaxation. With municipal support and cooperation with local music associations, we ensure that money or disabilities are no barriers and every child can join in.

239202.explanation: Progressief Bernheze finds sports associations important for health and social contact. If a club struggles, the municipality must help. This can be with money, but also advice, support, and removing rules so clubs can continue to exist.

239203.explanation: Progressief Bernheze does not want to cut spending on monument care. Monuments make our municipality beautiful and recognizable. If we spend less, special places may disappear. We want to work with owners to ensure monuments are well preserved.

239204.explanation: Progressief Bernheze finds safety important. Visible enforcement officers can contribute to a feeling of safety. We deploy more community enforcement officers only where needed and as a complement to police and welfare work, so we tackle problems smartly and affordably.

239205.explanation: Progressief Bernheze thinks it's important that police are visible and approachable. With a weekly consultation hour of the neighborhood officer in every village, residents can easily share their concerns. This helps address problems quickly and increases the feeling of safety.

239207.explanation: Progressief Bernheze believes that at least 30% social housing is needed in new construction. Currently it's only 19%. With 30% social housing, we reduce the huge waiting lists and ensure affordable housing in every community.

239208.explanation: Progressief Bernheze believes taller buildings are needed to address the housing shortage. With four floors or more, we can build homes quickly. Building higher keeps housing affordable.

239210.explanation: Progressief Bernheze believes holiday homes are meant for recreation. Permanent residence does not fit there. We want to solve the housing shortage with homes and other housing forms, such as tiny houses and seniors' cohousing, which are better suited for living in.

239212.explanation: Progressief Bernheze wants to make splitting homes and renting out single rooms easier. This way we use existing houses better and help reduce the housing shortage.

239213.explanation: Progressief Bernheze wants to help more people in poverty. Even in Bernheze, people struggle to make ends meet. Therefore, we want to provide clear help, such as income support and debt assistance, so no one falls through the cracks.

239214.explanation: People are not on social assistance because they don't want to work. They mainly need peace, trust, and a stable foundation. Progressief Bernheze wants to provide that. Extra obligations often make problems worse. Volunteering can be encouraged though.

239215.explanation: Progressief Bernheze believes debt forgiveness remains necessary for people with low incomes. Without forgiveness, they get into trouble. We want everyone to be able to pay their bills and avoid debts as much as possible.

239216.explanation: Progressief Bernheze wants to help people on welfare find work again. Sometimes education is necessary. By temporarily waiving the obligation to apply for jobs, people get the peace of mind to obtain their diploma and thus increase their chances of getting a job.

239218.explanation: Progressief Bernheze wants villages with more greenery and less concrete. By building fewer parking spaces in new developments, there is more room for plants and trees. We encourage people to use their bikes more often. This is healthier and better for the environment.

239220.explanation: Progressief Bernheze wants a 30 km/h speed limit within built-up areas. This makes streets safer and quieter. As a result, there are fewer accidents and children can play safely. Cyclists and pedestrians also feel more comfortable in the village.

239221.explanation: Progressief Bernheze wants care to be personal. People must be able to choose a care provider who fits their faith, culture, or conviction. This way, they feel understood, safe, and comfortable with the care they receive.

239222.explanation: Progressief Bernheze believes that quality in youth care is more important than the lowest price. Young people should receive help that truly fits their situation. Therefore, we choose good and reliable care, not just the cheapest provider.

239224.explanation: Progressief Bernheze wants a good recycling center close to residents. We cannot do without it. The current recycling center is outdated. Therefore, we will explore whether to renew it or to share recycling centers with neighboring municipalities.

239226.explanation: Progressief Bernheze wants to cooperate with associations based on trust. Together we make clear agreements about the rules. If those rules are not followed, the municipality intervenes. This keeps things fair for associations and the hospitality sector.

239227.explanation: Progressief Bernheze believes associations are important for our villages. Even small associations provide meeting points and social cohesion. Therefore, we want to continue supporting sports and cultural associations so everyone can participate and villages remain lively.

239236.explanation: Progressief Bernheze does not want to cut funding for village community centers. Village centers are important for contact and cohesion within the villages. They bring people together. We want these places to remain and the municipality to work closer to residents, also from village centers.

239237.explanation: Progressief Bernheze wants a service desk in every village where residents can go with questions. This must be a real place, not just a phone number. The desk is easily accessible and open every week so help is close to everyone.

239238.explanation: Progressief Bernheze considers health the most important priority. People can become ill if they live too close to a goat farm. Therefore, no new housing should be built within 1 km. This protects residents and prevents health problems.

239239.explanation: Progressief Bernheze wants enough social rental housing in every village. This way, starters and elderly can continue to live in their own village. By fairly distributing housing across all villages, we ensure strong and mixed neighborhoods where everyone can participate.

239241.explanation: Progressief Bernheze wants to give residents the space to start a home-based shop. Good ideas should receive help from the municipality. This is only possible if it fits in the neighborhood and does not cause nuisance. This way we strengthen the local economy and livability.

239242.explanation: Progressief Bernheze wants to help newcomers, not punish them. We work from trust and guidance. By supporting people with language, work, and social participation, we help them move forward faster than by cutting their benefits.

239243.explanation: Progressief Bernheze wants people to be able to cycle safely between villages. Therefore, the municipality must invest in good and safe bicycle routes. This encourages cycling instead of driving. It is healthy and better for the environment.

239244.explanation: Progressief Bernheze wants a 30 km/h speed limit within built-up areas for safety. Ring roads lie outside the built-up area and are intended for smooth traffic flow around the village. If the speed is too low there, traffic will still drive through the center.

239247.explanation: Progressief Bernheze wants to offer refugees a safe place. Bernheze takes responsibility in this. We choose the shelter location together with residents and it must be a pleasant place to live. More than bed, bread, and bath is needed: room for contact, work, sports, learning, and privacy.

239248.explanation: Progressief Bernheze wants to offer refugees a safe place. By choosing multiple small shelter locations and good consultation with residents, we ensure a welcoming reception and a good start for newcomers in our municipality.

239185.explanation: ONS Bernheze firmly stands for an independent Bernheze. We believe Bernheze is strong enough to make choices independently, stay close to residents, and provide customized solutions. Cooperation with neighboring municipalities where useful: yes. Forced municipal mergers: no.

239186.explanation: Residents must have real influence. For major decisions – such as large spatial plans or municipal reorganization – direct consultation must be possible. We see a corrective binding referendum as a supplement to the municipal council: residents get the final say on major decisions.

239187.explanation: If residents or associations can do something as well, better, cheaper, or with more involvement, they should be allowed to do so. The municipality facilitates, supports, and remains ultimately responsible, but allows room for initiative and ownership.

239190.explanation: The current regulations and exemption possibilities are sufficient.

239191.explanation: Partially agree. ONS Bernheze chooses balance. Nature development is important, but not at the expense of the agricultural sector as a whole. Voluntariness, customization, and linking to landscape enhancement are leading – no generic policy that displaces farmers.

239192.explanation: ONS Bernheze supports ancillary activities on farms, but not automatically and not always. Customization is essential here. Activities must fit the environment, be traffic-safe, and have local support. A statement that says “always allow” therefore does not align with our vision of a thoughtful but also responsible municipality.

239193.explanation: Local energy initiatives deserve support. Especially when residents invest together and the proceeds stay local. ONS Bernheze sees this as an opportunity for support and affordability.

239195.explanation: Effective integration is important, but funds must be used purposefully. Focus on language, work, and social participation. No extra budgets without demonstrable effect.

239197.explanation: Air quality in Bernheze and the surroundings must be improved for the general health of young and old.

239198.explanation: ONS Bernheze is very cautious about solar panels on good farmland and is basically against it. First, roofs, business parks, and other suitable locations must be fully utilized. Exceptions are only conceivable with proven added value and local support.

239199.explanation: Sports must be accessible to everyone. Through programs like the Youth Fund for Sports & Culture, ONS Bernheze wants to enable participation without stigma.

239201.explanation: Cultural education is valuable. ONS Bernheze supports music education through schools and associations, but chooses cooperation and reach, not individual lessons for every child.

239202.explanation: Sports clubs play an important social role. ONS Bernheze believes the municipality can financially contribute in emergencies, sustainability efforts, or temporary problems. We do expect clubs to take responsibility and cooperate.

239203.explanation: Monuments are the memory of Bernheze. ONS Bernheze sees monument care as an investment in identity, tourism, and livability – certainly also for local initiatives and heritage.

239204.explanation: ONS Bernheze believes effectiveness, visibility, and cooperation are more important than numbers alone. More enforcement officers is not a goal in itself; expansion is only desirable where there is proven necessity and clear added value.

239205.explanation: Visibility and accessibility increase the feeling of safety. ONS Bernheze wants to actively address this with the police.

239207.explanation: Strive for growth in social rental housing, but with flexibility per village and project. Adhering rigidly to one percentage can have adverse effects.

239208.explanation: High-rise buildings remain limited (the principle is a maximum of 3 floors above ground) and fitting the village character.

239210.explanation: We utilize all possibilities, including permanent residence on recreation parks.

239212.explanation: We utilize all possibilities, also including room rental and hospitable renting; shared housing and splitting properties.

239213.explanation: To prevent worse, we consider exceptional assistance as a gift when repayment is unrealistic; generous cancellation of municipal charges; regulation-light social assistance with room for additional income and introduction of hardship clauses where possible.

239214.explanation: First and foremost, looking for paid work. Participate where possible, no coercion with medical or social barriers.

239215.explanation: This is necessary to prevent poverty and related problems from worsening.

239216.explanation: Education increases the chance of (permanent) employment.

239218.explanation: We are moving towards more, so two parking spaces per home.

239220.explanation: Residential streets yes, main roads not automatically.

239221.explanation: We take personal wishes into account where possible, but good, accessible, respectful care for everyone comes first.

239224.explanation: We want residents to be able to go to the environmental site of the nearby neighboring municipality for better service and waste separation.

239226.explanation: No patronizing, but enforcement in case of abuse or nuisance.

239227.explanation: Among others, associations keep villages livable.

239236.explanation: They form the social and cultural heart of the village.

239237.explanation: We will expand opportunities for our residents by keeping the town hall open every weekday from 9:00 AM to 7:00 PM and allowing walk-ins without appointment for assistance.

239238.explanation: Due to health risks, the municipality must be very cautious with new homes near goat farms and expansion of the farm.

239239.explanation: Social rental homes are needed in all villages, but distribution must be based on the needs and possibilities per village, not on a fixed percentage.

239241.explanation: Only where facilities are lacking, and therefore only in the villages of Loosbroek and Vorstenbosch to strengthen livability.

239242.explanation: First encourage, and if people do not want to, then sanction.

239243.explanation: We want safe cycling connections between the villages.

239247.explanation: Small-scale centers for local residents and asylum seekers who need temporary housing, depending on the population of the directly involved village (maximum 50 to 75 residents). Our smallest villages Loosbroek and Vorstenbosch are excluded.

239248.explanation: Small-scale centers for local residents and asylum seekers who need temporary housing, depending on the population of the directly involved village (maximum 50 to 75 residents). Our smallest villages Loosbroek and Vorstenbosch are excluded.

239185.explanation: Bernheze must remain an independent municipality. We want to stay in control of our own future. Collaboration with other municipalities is fine, but we do not want the city to decide what happens in our villages. Our own culture is too important for that.

239186.explanation: Good politics starts with listening to the residents. We do not want to make decisions without residents having a say. That is why we want a referendum. This way, people decide for themselves about their own neighborhood.

239187.explanation: We encourage residents to make their own plans for their neighborhood. If you want to take responsibility yourself, we are happy to help. It is important that the money is spent well and that the organization of the plan is correct. This way, we ensure a good result together.

239190.explanation: We believe hospitality entrepreneurs should have the freedom to determine their own opening hours, as long as they demonstrate being a good neighbor. The focus must be on tackling nuisance: complaints about noise, waste, or parking must be taken seriously and the municipality must intervene firmly in case of problems. No nuisance means no restrictions on opening hours in our view.

239191.explanation: The farmer must always agree on this. The municipality must first carefully examine the land. Perhaps the land is better suited for building new homes. Because we urgently need new housing.

239192.explanation: Farmers must be given space to expand their businesses on their own land. For example, a small shop or a campsite should be possible. We do consider how big the plan is and exactly what the farmer wants to do.

239193.explanation: Clean energy projects must earn back their own money. If a plan makes a profit, the municipality does not need to provide funding. The entrepreneur can then finance the project themselves.

239195.explanation: CRB finds it very important that newcomers adapt and participate in our municipality. We want to help with that. But we also expect newcomers to make an effort. They are required to participate, and we want to verify that this actually happens.

239197.explanation: We believe that the use of wood stoves and fireplaces should remain permitted. It is important that burning is done in a safe and clean way. We do not want this to cause too much nuisance in the neighborhood.

239198.explanation: We want to use agricultural land for agriculture, nature, or new homes. Solar panels belong on the roofs of houses and businesses. We do not want large solar parks in nature or in the countryside.

239199.explanation: We find sports very important. But there are enough ways to stay active without the municipality having to pay for it. Therefore, the municipality does not have to reimburse sports club memberships.

239201.explanation: We find music lessons at school very valuable. But schools should pay for this themselves from their own budget. We believe the municipality does not need to provide extra money per child for this.

239202.explanation: We believe associations must remain healthy by coming up with new ideas themselves. It is not the municipality’s task to save associations that have not managed their finances properly.

239203.explanation: We want to spend less money on monuments. The municipality should only pay for maintenance that is really necessary to preserve important monuments. This prevents unnecessary costs.

239204.explanation: We want to deploy more enforcement officers (BOA's) to improve safety in our municipality. We will only do this if the law gives them enough authority. They must be able to perform their work well and safely.

239205.explanation: Safety in our region is very important to us. We want the community police officer to hold a consultation hour every week. This makes it easier for residents to drop by for a conversation. This leads to better contact between people and the police.

239207.explanation: There is a great need for affordable homes for starters and homes for elderly people where they can live for a long time. We want 30% of new homes to be social rental housing. This helps ensure enough affordable homes.

239208.explanation: We believe buildings up to four stories are a good solution for the housing shortage. By building higher, we can put more homes on less land. This gives especially young people the chance to stay living in their own village.

239210.explanation: We want people to be allowed to live in a holiday home all year round. This is a smart, temporary solution to the housing shortage. As long as there is a housing shortage, we allow it. Only when there are enough homes again will we reconsider.

239212.explanation: Renting out rooms helps combat the housing shortage. We see this as a good idea, as long as the house is safe and the kitchen and bathroom are clean. This way, we can quickly provide more people with a place to live.

239213.explanation: We believe the safety net for people in poverty is more than sufficient. Sometimes it is even too much. Because of this, people sometimes travel through four safe countries to apply for asylum in the Netherlands. It also means that people are not motivated to work and contribute to our municipality.

239214.explanation: Nothing comes for free. We believe that those who receive welfare benefits should give something back to the community. If you can work, you should do meaningful work for society. This way, everyone contributes their part.

239215.explanation: Everyone uses the facilities provided by the municipality. Therefore, we believe that everyone should pay taxes, regardless of whether their income is high or low. This way, every resident contributes fairly to our municipality.

239216.explanation: Applying for jobs is no longer very difficult nowadays. We believe that people receiving benefits should be required to continue looking for work. They can combine this well with a course or training. This increases their chances of finding a job.

239218.explanation: If we build more and encourage work, more parking spaces will be needed. Families often have more cars. We want there to be enough space for all those cars on the street. This keeps the neighborhoods organized and accessible for everyone.

239220.explanation: We are against a uniform 30 kilometers per hour rule for all built-up areas. We want to consider the best speed per street. In some roads, 30 kilometers per hour is unnecessary, but in other places it is very important for safety.

239221.explanation: Care providers are professionals who do their work well. There is currently a great shortage of personnel in healthcare. Therefore, we believe that people should not be refused care because of their religion or culture. Everyone who needs care should be helped by the available professionals.

239222.explanation: The municipality must pay close attention to the costs of youth care. But the quality of care for our children is paramount. Cuts should never result in children not receiving the help they need.

239224.explanation: We want to decide ourselves how we manage our waste. Ideally, we want an additional recycling center in our own municipality. This way, residents have to drive less far with their waste. It prevents littering in nature and is easier for our people.

239226.explanation: We believe that sports canteens are not meant for private parties such as weddings. These buildings are paid for with municipal money. Therefore, they should not create unfair competition for local entrepreneurs and hospitality businesses.

239227.explanation: Clubs and associations must be fundamentally healthy through the involvement of their own members. We believe a club must have enough members to exist. Therefore, we want to stop providing fixed annual funding to very small associations.

239236.explanation: We want to critically examine how much money we spend on village halls. If a village hall is hardly used anymore, it costs the municipality too much money. The costs must be reasonable compared to what the building provides for the residents.

239237.explanation: Not everyone can handle computers or the internet well, especially many elderly people. We believe that important municipal information should not be available online only. You should also be able to get help at a counter, via brochures, or in the newspaper.

239238.explanation: We must use every opportunity to build new homes. If residents are honestly informed beforehand about the risks, they should be free to choose whether they want to live near a goat farm. It is then their own responsibility.

239239.explanation: We want to build houses wherever possible. It is a good goal to distribute new homes fairly across all villages. But this should not delay construction. Speed is now more important because the housing need is very high.

239241.explanation: In principle, we agree with the statement, but we draw a clear line at the impact on livability. Small-scale sales of, for example, local pottery or a small home business should definitely be allowed. The situation changes when a business causes daily traffic or large transports, such as an online clothing store or a PostNL point. Therefore, our approval is conditional: permits are required so the municipality can critically monitor the balance between business activity and residential enjoyment.

239242.explanation: Learning the Dutch language is the most important step to participate in our society. We believe newcomers are required to take language lessons. If they refuse, we want to reduce their benefits. This is a fair incentive to really engage in integration.

239243.explanation: More money is not needed for new bike routes. What we do find very important is that the existing bike paths remain good and safe. Therefore, the municipality must keep sufficient budget for maintaining the paths we already have.

239244.explanation: We want to consider the best speed per ring road. Sometimes 50 km/h hinders traffic flow, while elsewhere it is safer. We choose a tailored approach: in each area, we look at what is needed for safety and good flow. No unnecessary 'one-size-fits-all' solutions.

239247.explanation: There is no support for an asylum seeker center (AZC) in Bernheze. CRB is against an AZC in Bernheze and also at the Zevenbergen 10 location. If it does come, it must be on our terms and with guarantees. Controllable intake, mandatory integration, and a safe environment are our highest priorities.

239248.explanation: In forced asylum seeker accommodation, CRB resolutely chooses small scale. Even 75 people at one location is too large for us. We advocate for even smaller locations. This prevents problems and keeps our villages safe, controllable, and livable for everyone.

statements.title: The municipality should make it possible for residents to start a shop from home.
The maximum speed limit in built-up areas should be set to 30 km/h.
The municipality should spend more money on integrating newcomers in Bernheze.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet meer geld uitgeven aan

titleParts.glossary: Newcomers learn the language and customs of the Netherlands.
People who are new to the Netherlands.

statements.theme: Closing times for bars and restaurants
statements.title: Cafés and restaurants must be able to determine their own opening hours.
The municipality should support more people living in poverty.
Residents of Bernheze should be able to take part in decision-making on important issues via a referendum.

titleParts.text: De inwoners van Bernheze moeten over belangrijke onderwerpen mee kunnen beslissen via een

titleParts.glossary: A public consultation where residents can express their opinion on a political issue. The municipal council is not obliged to follow the result; legally it cannot.

statements.title: The municipality should create fewer parking spaces in new residential areas.
More residential apartment buildings of four stories or more may be allowed in Bernheze.
The municipality should ban solar panels on agricultural land.

statements.theme: Exemption from the job application obligation
statements.title: People on social assistance who are taking additional training should temporarily not have to apply for jobs.
The municipality must allow holiday homes to be used as a permanent residence.
Bernheze should have more municipal enforcement officers (BOAs) focused on safety.

titleParts.glossary: People who, for the municipality, check whether citizens and businesses comply with the rules.

titleParts.text: (boa’s) komen die zich met veiligheid bezighouden.

statements.title: Residents with questions for the municipality should be able to visit a local municipal service desk in every village.
A maximum speed of 50 km/h should apply on the future ring roads.
statements.moreInfo: The municipality plans to build ring roads near Heeswijk-Dinther and Nistelrode; the municipal council still has to decide. The normal maximum speed outside built-up areas is 80 km/h.

statements.title: People receiving social assistance should, in return, do socially useful work.
Several small reception locations for asylum seekers should be established (maximum 75 people per location).

titleParts.text: Er moeten meerdere kleine opvanglocaties voor

titleParts.glossary: People who have applied for asylum and are still waiting for a decision.

statements.moreInfo: In July 2024 the municipal council decided that Bernheze will take in a maximum of 250 asylum seekers. The council still needs to decide on the location for housing these asylum seekers.

statements.title: In Bernheze the use of open fireplaces and wood stoves should remain permitted.
When building social rental homes, the municipality should ensure an equal distribution across all villages.

titleParts.glossary: A rental home that at the start of the tenancy does not cost more than a certain amount. In 2025 that amount is €900.07 per month.

titleParts.text: moet de gemeente zorgen voor een gelijke verdeling over alle dorpen.

statements.title: Residents who want to generate sustainable energy together should receive financial support from the municipality.

titleParts.glossary: Energy based on resources that are effectively infinite, such as sunlight and wind.

titleParts.text: op willen wekken, moeten financiële steun krijgen van de gemeente.

statements.title: The municipality should reduce social assistance benefits for newcomers who do not attend language classes.

titleParts.glossary: People who are new to the Netherlands.

titleParts.text: die geen taalles volgen, korten op hun bijstandsuitkering.

statements.title: People who need care should always be able to choose a caregiver who matches their religion, culture, or beliefs.
The municipality should stop forgiving municipal taxes for low-income residents.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet stoppen met het

titleParts.glossary: That people no longer have to pay for something.

titleParts.text: van de gemeentelijke belastingen voor inwoners met een laag inkomen.

statements.title: At least 30% of newly built homes in Bernheze should be social rental housing. Currently about 19% of all homes in the municipality are social rental.

titleParts.text: Van de nieuw te bouwen woningen in Bernheze moet minimaal 30% een

titleParts.glossary: A rental home with a low rent (in 2026 at most €932.93 per month) for people with a low income.

titleParts.text: zijn. Nu is ongeveer 19% van alle woningen in de gemeente sociale huur.

statements.title: The municipality should spend more money on safe bicycle routes between the villages.
If sports clubs are struggling, the municipality should contribute financially.

titleParts.text: Als sportverenigingen het moeilijk hebben, moet de gemeente

statements.title: The municipality must ask the police to hold a weekly meeting with the community police officer in each village.
Every primary school child should be able to receive music lessons subsidized by the municipality.

titleParts.glossary: Money the government gives to citizens, businesses, and organizations to support them.

titleParts.text: van de gemeente muziekles kunnen krijgen.

statements.title: The municipality should cut spending on village community centers.
Residents should be able to take over the management of, for example, a sports hall or a public green space from the municipality.
The municipality should not allow new homes within a 1 km radius of goat farms.
statements.moreInfo: Research shows that people living close to a goat farm have a higher risk of pneumonia. The national government wants rules for a minimum distance between goat farms and new homes to protect residents' health. The Health Council advises a distance of 1 kilometer.

shootoutStatements.title: Bernheze must remain an independent municipality.
The municipality must check whether sports canteens are being used for general parties or weddings.
The municipality must encourage converting agricultural land in Bernheze into nature reserves.
The asylum seekers' centre must be located at Zevenbergen 10 in Nistelrode.
The municipality must stop giving subsidies to associations with very few members.
The municipality must pay sports club membership fees for people with little money.
The municipality should always allow farmers to open, for example, a shop or a campsite at their farm.
The recycling centre in Bernheze must close.
The municipality should allow landlords to rent out individual rooms instead of entire houses.
The municipality should cut spending on heritage conservation.
When selecting organizations for youth care, the municipality should primarily pay attention to price.
